title: review-envelope
description: The ONE canonical reviewer-output schema for the AI-native SDLC. Every reviewer (task-reviewer, the lens specialists, integration-reviewer, spec-reviewer) emits exactly this shape. The deterministic engine (.claude/workflows/execute-spec.js) inlines an equivalent SUBSET (additionalProperties:true, so the extra fields here still validate). There is NO top-level verdict a reviewer self-reports: a malformed/absent envelope is a contract violation; an abstaining reviewer sets reviewer_status:abstained (the orchestrator escalates, never accepts). See review-primitives.md for the severity spine, grounding rules, and severity->action policy.

reviewer_status.description: assessed = the reviewer graded the artifact (default). abstained = could NOT assess; the orchestrator MUST escalate, never accept — even with findings: [].

severity.description: The severity ladder. blocker/major route to fix_loop; nit/suggestion route to batch_followup_and_accept.

criterion.description: The grounded citation — MUST start with an allowed prefix for this reviewer's role (see review-primitives.md > Grounding rules). The canonical PR-side prefix set (per review-primitives.md > PR-side canonical prefix table, mirrored by execute-spec.js ALLOWED_PREFIX) is enumerated here as a machine-parseable list — PR_SIDE_PREFIXES = [ac:, adr:, std:, inv:, design:, lens:, monorepo:, task:, spec:]. Example citations: ac:AC-NNN, adr:ADR-NNN, std:<section-anchor>, inv:<INV-ID>, design:<token-or-component>, lens:<lens-name>, monorepo:boundary, task:scope, spec:<gap-kind>. Spec-side reviewers use the distinct spec-side prefix set.

citation.description: Alias of criterion; criterion is canonical (the policy reads criterion, falling back to citation).

location.description: file:line (PR findings) or spec section heading (spec findings).

suggested_fix.description: One sentence: what to do; null if unknown.

lens.description: The lens this finding came from (a multi-lens single-pass reviewer MUST set it so a scoped fix re-review can target the lens; a single-lens reviewer may omit it).

altitude.description: design = the spec/plan is wrong (escalate to replan/amend, no code edit can satisfy it); implementation = the code is wrong (enters the bounded fix loop). Absent => implementation (conservative default).

verification.description: PR-side: the commands the reviewer confirmed were run + their result. Spec-side: null.
